accepted.reason: Total-variation proximal routines are strong optimization kernels with nested passes over vectors/matrices.
Sigma estimation via finite differences/MSE is a compact numeric loop workload suitable for speedup tasks.
Primality and sieve routines are canonical algorithmic kernels with clear optimization signal.
Min/max scan plus per-pixel normalization is a standard image-processing loop pattern.
Shortest-path computation over weighted graphs is a high-value algorithmic optimization target.
Entity patch/deletion decode flow provides useful stateful list-processing logic for agentic coding tasks.

skipped.reason: Contains legacy Python2-style code and heavy project-specific statistical context, reducing portability and quality.
Profiling-library wrapper is systems integration glue rather than a standalone compute optimization kernel.
Randomized itemset-hiding heuristic is highly domain-specific and less stable as generic optimization training data.
Streaming/msgpack merge utility is IO-oriented and orchestration-heavy with limited Cython optimization relevance.
